title: C File Handling
content:
### File Handling in C

File handling in C uses the `FILE` type from `<stdio.h>` to read from and write to files.

### File Operations

| Operation | Function |
|-----------|----------|
| Open file | `fopen()` |
| Close file | `fclose()` |
| Read character | `fgetc()` / `getc()` |
| Write character | `fputc()` / `putc()` |
| Read string | `fgets()` |
| Write string | `fputs()` |
| Read formatted | `fscanf()` |
| Write formatted | `fprintf()` |
| Read binary | `fread()` |
| Write binary | `fwrite()` |
| Check end of file | `feof()` |
| Error checking | `ferror()` |

### File Pointer

```c
FILE *filePointer;
filePointer = fopen("filename", "mode");
```

---

> **Key Point:** Always check if `fopen()` returns NULL before using the file pointer. Always close files with `fclose()` to flush buffers and free resources.

quiz.question: What does fopen() return on failure?
